name: Blue and Green Lakes Loop
name_original: Entre le lac bleu et le lac vert de Léognan
description: Hike 6.4 miles (10.3 km) between the serene Blue Lake and Green Lake of Léognan, exploring forests and vineyards on a moderate trail.
region_intro: Hiking around Léognan offers diverse landscapes characterized by extensive vineyards, dense forests, and tranquil lakes. The region, situated south of Bordeaux, features gravelly soils typical of its renowned wine-growing appellation. Numerous streams, including the Eau Blanche, meander through the area, adding to the natural features. The terrain generally presents gentle elevation changes, making it accessible for various hiking abilities. Best hiking trails around Léognan The most popular hiking route is Blue and Green Lakes Loop, a 6.4 miles (10.3 km) moderate trail that takes 2 hours 37 minutes to complete. This route explores both Lac Bleu and Green Lake, winding through forests and vineyards. Another top favourite among local hikers is Green Lake – Cayac Priory loop from Léognan, a difficult 13.4 miles (21.6 km) path. This trail leads through varied terrain, including forests and vineyards, and passes by the historic Cayac Priory. Local hikers also love the Blue Lake loop from Léognan, a 6.1 miles (9.9 km) trail leading through the Lac Bleu area, often completed in about 2 hours 32 minutes. Hiking around Léognan is defined by its vineyards, forests, and serene lakes, with trails often following streams. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging routes. The routes in Léognan are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 250 reviews. More than 1400 hikers have used komoot to explore Léognan's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Léognan?
A: Léognan offers a diverse landscape characterized by extensive vineyards, dense forests, and tranquil lakes. You'll find paths winding through the renowned Pessac-Léognan appellation, past prestigious wine estates, and through 'green lung' pine forests like the Lac Bleu Forest Park. The region is also home to serene bodies of water such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1303316" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Blue Lake&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2899316" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Green Lake&lt;/a&gt;, and trails often follow the meandering Eau Blanche stream.

Q: Are there many easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or leisurely strolls in Léognan?
A: Yes, Léognan offers a good selection of easy trails. Out of the 36 hiking routes available, 14 are classified as easy. A great option for a leisurely walk is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10706904" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Blue Lake – Blue Lake loop from Léognan&lt;/a&gt;, which is approximately 7.0 km long and takes about 2 hours to complete, exploring the beautiful Lac Bleu area.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Léognan?
A: Absolutely. Many of Léognan's trails are designed as circular routes, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Popular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e838213153/blue-and-green-lakes-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Blue and Green Lakes Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through forests and vineyards, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15218240" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Blue Lake loop from Léognan&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I discover on hikes around Léognan?
A: Léognan's hikes are rich with natural beauty and interesting landmarks. You can explore the clear waters of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1303316" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Blue Lake&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2899316" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Green Lake&lt;/a&gt;, which are former gravel pits now leisure areas. Trails also lead through extensive vineyards, past prestigious châteaux like La Louvière and Carbonnieux, and along the tranquil Eau Blanche stream. The Parc forestier du Lac Bleu is another highlight, offering lush pine forests.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Léognan?
A: Yes, Léognan has several parks and trails suitable for families. The Parc forestier du Lac Bleu is an excellent choice, offering numerous circuits across 18 hectares that are accessible year-round and perfect for picnics and nature walks. Many of the easy and moderate trails, especially those around the lakes, are also well-suited for families.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Léognan?
A: The hiking trails in Léognan are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.6 stars from over 250 reviews. Hikers often praise the region's unique blend of vineyards, forests, and serene lakes, as well as the well-maintained paths that offer a variety of experiences for different skill levels.

Q: Are there any trails that pass by vineyards or châteaux in Léognan?
A: Yes, Léognan is at the heart of the Pessac-Léognan wine appellation, so many trails wind through vineyards and past historic châteaux. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20694637" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Château La Louvière – Château Haut-Bailly loop from Léognan&lt;/a&gt; is a great example, offering views of these renowned wine estates. Other routes, like the 'Boucle des châteaux de Léognan' (not a komoot route in this guide, but mentioned in region research), specifically highlight these iconic landmarks.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Léognan?
A: Léognan offers pleasant hiking conditions throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn are particularly beautiful, with comfortable temperatures for walking through vineyards and forests. The Lac Bleu Forest Park is accessible year-round, providing a 'green lung of freshness' even during warmer months, thanks to its dense tree cover.

Q: How many hiking trails are available in Léognan?
A: There are over 35 hiking trails available in Léognan, catering to various preferences and fitness levels. This includes 14 easy routes, 20 moderate routes, and 2 more challenging options, ensuring there's something for everyone.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging hikes in the Léognan area?
A: For those seeking a more challenging experience, Léognan does offer longer routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20696764" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Green Lake – Cayac Priory loop from Léognan&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 21.6 km path that leads through varied terrain, including forests and vineyards, and passes by the historic Cayac Priory.

Q: What kind of elevation changes can I expect on Léognan's hiking trails?
A: The terrain in Léognan generally presents gentle elevation changes, making it accessible for a wide range of hiking abilities. While some longer routes may have more cumulative ascent, most trails feature relatively flat or gently rolling landscapes, particularly those winding through vineyards and around the lakes.
tips: Lake with space to fill its cans, eat a bit and a few paths around.

The Green Lake is a body of water about twenty kilometers from Bordeaux in the town of Canéjan. Fishing on this 7.5 hectare body of water is possible with a fishing license. Many species of fish are well represented in Lac Vert, including pike, smallmouth bass, carp or roach. The local fishing club regularly organizes trout releases.

faqs.question: How difficult is the hike between the Blue Lake and Green Lake of Léognan?
faqs.answer: This trail is generally considered moderate. While some sections are easily accessible, the full 10.3 km loop can take around 2 hours and 37 minutes, suggesting a good level of fitness is beneficial. The path is mostly accessible, but sure-footedness may be required in certain areas.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter a varied terrain, including unpaved paths, natural trails, and gravel sections. There are also some paved or asphalt stretches, such as municipal roads and cycle paths, guiding you through picturesque forests and vineyards.

faqs.question: What are the main natural features or landmarks along the way?
faqs.answer: The primary attractions are the two beautiful lakes themselves: the Blue Lake of Léognan and the Green Lake of Canéjan. The route also takes you through charming forests and vineyards, characteristic of the Léognan area.

faqs.question: Is the trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through vineyards, near wildlife, or on shared paths with cyclists and other hikers.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The trail is enjoyable in most seasons. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and beautiful scenery with changing foliage or blooming flora. Summer can be warm, so an early start is advisable. Winter hikes are also possible, though some paths might be muddy after rain.

faqs.question: Where can I park and start the hike?
faqs.answer: The trail is known for its accessibility, with starting points often located near parking lots. Specific parking information can vary, but generally, you can find convenient parking close to the lakes or along the associated "Graves Route - Blue Loop" in the Léognan area.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail or the Parcours des Graves?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no permits or entrance fees required to hike this trail or access the general area of the Parcours des Graves. It's freely accessible for outdoor enthusiasts.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is part of the broader "Graves Route - Blue Loop" and intersects with several local paths. You'll find yourself on sections of the Rocade de Bordeaux, Chemin de la Braneyre, Chemin du Peych, Chemin du Prieur, and various Chemin rural paths.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restrooms along the trail?
faqs.answer: While the trail itself winds through natural areas, the Léognan region offers amenities. You might find cafes, restaurants, and restrooms in the nearby towns. It's always a good idea to bring your own water and snacks, especially for the main hiking sections.

faqs.question: What makes the Blue Lake and Green Lake unique?
faqs.answer: The lakes are notable for the striking colors of their water, which give them their names. The Blue Lake is a peaceful oasis with white sand borders, ideal for picnics and birdwatching. The Green Lake, a former gravel quarry, is a 12-hectare body of water also popular with walkers and joggers.

faqs.question: Is this a circular hike?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route between the Blue Lake and Green Lake of Léognan is typically enjoyed as a circular hike, allowing you to experience the diverse landscapes and return to your starting point without retracing your steps.
